sanskrit: सन्तुष्टः सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चयः । मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥
hindiTranslation: सब प्राणियोंमें द्वेषभावसे रहित, सबका मित्र प्रेमी और दयालु, ममतारहित, अहंकाररहित, सुखदुःखकी प्राप्तिमें सम, क्षमाशील, निरन्तर सन्तुष्ट,योगी, शरीरको वशमें किये हुए, दृढ़ निश्चयवाला, मेरेमें अर्पित मनबुद्धिवाला जो मेरा भक्त है, वह मेरेको प्रिय है ॥
englishTranslation: The devoted yogi is always content, self-controlled, and steady in resolve. One whose mind and discerning mind are offered to Me is dear to Me.
essence: Real devotion is a steady mind already placed beyond itself.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A true devotee stays content, controls the body and mind, keeps firm resolve, and places both mind and discernment in Krishna.
simpleInsight: Devotion is not only feeling; it becomes a steady way of living that makes the heart simple and strong.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna stands frozen while Krishna continues shaping a path of devotion. After describing kindness, freedom from hatred, and equality, Krishna names the devotee who has become inwardly steady: content, disciplined, resolved, and fully offered to Him.
modernRelevance: You are exhausted, but you still choose not to spiral, not to blame, not to scatter yourself. Something in you stays gathered, and that quiet steadiness is its own devotion.
translationLiteral: सन्तुष्टः (content) / सततम् (always) / योगी (yogi) / यतात्मा (self-controlled) / दृढनिश्चयः (firm in resolve) / मयि (in Me) / अर्पित (offered) / मनः (mind) / बुद्धिः (discerning mind) / यः (who) / मत्-भक्तः (My devotee) / सः (that one) / मे (to Me) / प्रियः (dear)
insight: It shifts devotion from emotion alone to a disciplined, inwardly gathered life.

quoteVariants.text: Be content, steady, and offered to the divine. Such devotion is dear.
Content. Steady. Offered to the divine.
A steady heart is dearer than noisy devotion.
Stay content. Offer mind and resolve.
